Patch Set 1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

(1 inline comment)

It looks reasonable to me, but I wouldn't mind if Mike has time to look at it so that he can flag any issues that might be resolved before landing on master to simplify merge into the Orion branch, and any issues w.r.t. OSD API.

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(3 inline comments)

This looks OK in general, but patch must be re-freshed against the latest tree to be landed clearly and wiretests modifications must be done in proper way. See inlines.

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(2 inline comments)

Patch Set 1:

This patch appears mostly duplicated with Change 1194 and should be abandoned, unless you are going to use it for the server-side support of wide striping?

Patch Set 1:

Yes I plan to turn this patch into the server side work.

Patch Set 5: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

You removed some fixes for the user tools. You removed the reallocation of the ost uuid buffers in llapi_poollist and in other places. Instead you hard coded it to 2000.Personally I like to see any limits be done away with.Instead we use what the file system tells use is the ost count. The lfs_find fixes a memory leak.

Patch Set 5:

Yes, but patch is for the large stripe support, it doesn`t relate to improving user tools or fixes. My point is less and clear patch for the large stripe support. 200 lines less from first version. If you want to do improvement or fix for tools, pls submit another patch.

Patch Set 5: No score

Okay, I understand know. Just confused by things like INIT_ALLOC_NUM_OSTS which I do have a separate patch for. Would you might if we had  aversion of this patch with no changes to liblustreapi.c or liblustreapi.h

commit.subject: LU-80 lov: large stripe count support
commit.message:
LU-80 lov: large stripe count support

Currently a file can be stripped across OSTs up to a limit of
160 stripes. This patch expands that limit to 2000 and it is
possible to go even to larger stripe counts.

Signed-off-by: Alexander Boyko <alexander_boyko@xyratex.com>
Change-Id: I42e1aad35dd056faac23a0d5b025e0a23fc4ec2f
